v3 direction memo · internal · 15 May 2026
For founders & brand · From design · Re Arda v3 visual direction

A sovereign brand needs a sovereign look.

The wordmark and target mark are now locked. This memo proposes a tightened palette — navy plus one decisive partner — and a working theory for imagery that earns its place on the page. Three things to decide. Four directions to react to. Two pages.

Constants
Navy 900 · Inter Tight · sentence case
Under review
Secondary palette · imagery
Out of scope today
Typography · iconography · motion
§01 / 03   The logo

A target, a wordmark, one safe area.

The new mark is a four-point compass star inside split brackets — a target the operator has aligned. It carries the brand at small sizes; the wordmark carries it at large. The two travel together, except where the mark stands alone.

Primary lockup · white on navy 1000
Mark only · favicon, header, share
28px header
48px footer

Don't

No gradient grounds
No rotation, ever
No squeeze, no stretch

Clearspace equals the height of the bracket. The mark never appears on photography directly — it sits on a navy or paper ground.

§02 / 03   Secondary palette

Navy is the constant. One partner does the work.

Each direction below pairs navy with a single load-bearing colour and a supporting neutral. Same mock applied four ways so you can read the difference, not the description. We pick one. Worm green and worm red survive in every direction — they're load-bearing semantics, not decoration.

A

Receipt

Audit-grade · paper trail · FT-leaning

A warm cream paper takes the role white used to play. Navy sits on it like ink on a notarised page. The single saturated accent is a sharp Citadel coral — used once per surface, on the claim that earns it. This is the brand for the audit committee. Says: we are documents you can defend.

900
Navy
0A1224
paper
Paper
F1E9D6
coral
Coral
DC3D2A
ink
Ink
1A1612
warm
Warm
857C6A
ARDA
PlatformDeploymentEvidenceTrust
Sovereign AI · regulated workloads

AI you can defend
to your regulator.

Own, operate, govern — inside your trust boundary, with the audit trail your CISO expects.

04
Deployment options
100%
In your boundary
AESCSF
CPS 234 · ISO 27001
B

Sodium

OT · substation · utilities-grade

The colour of substation lamps and high-voltage warning signs. A saturated sodium amber on deep navy reads as operational infrastructure — the kind of brand a dispatch deck would believe. Warmer than blue-on-blue, more specific than orange-on-anything. Says: we belong on the plant floor.

900
Navy
0A1224
sodium
Sodium
F5A623
chalk
Chalk
EAE6D6
red
Red
C8311B
slate
Steel
786E61
ARDA
PlatformDeploymentEvidenceTrust
Sovereign AI · regulated workloads

AI you can defend
to your regulator.

Own, operate, govern — inside your trust boundary, with the audit trail your CISO expects.

04
Deployment options
100%
In your boundary
AESCSF
CPS 234 · ISO 27001
C

Treaty

Institutional · regulator-side · old money

Navy plus a deep Oxford green on bone paper. Reads like a constitutional document — the brand of the people who write the rules, not the people selling around them. Quiet, expensive, undeniably grown-up. Closest cousin: Bloomberg, the FT magazine, college presses. Says: we belong in the regulator's office.

900
Navy
0A1224
oxford
Oxford
1F5440
bone
Bone
EFEAD8
brass
Brass
A18656
slate
Stone
6B6B5F
ARDA
PlatformDeploymentEvidenceTrust
Sovereign AI · regulated workloads

AI you can defend
to your regulator.

Own, operate, govern — inside your trust boundary, with the audit trail your CISO expects.

04
Deployment options
100%
In your boundary
AESCSF
CPS 234 · ISO 27001
D

Static

Engineering tool · dev-team-first · Linear-adjacent

A vivid electric lime against deep navy. The brand of a tool engineers actually use — terminal-feeling, slightly radioactive, completely unmistakable from a Mistral or an Oracle. Most divergent of the four. Risk: reads younger than our CIO buyer. Reward: gets emails opened. Says: this was built by people who ship.

900
Navy

lime

Sovereign AI · regulated workloads

AI you can defend
to your regulator.

Own, operate, govern — inside your trust boundary, with the audit trail your CISO expects.

04
Deployment options
100%
In your boundary
AESCSF
CPS 234 · ISO 27001

Design's lean: Receipt (A) for the marketing surface and exec comms; Treaty (C) as the close runner-up if you want the brand to read older. Sodium and Static are both real options but pull the brand in a more vertical-specific direction.

§03 / 03   Imagery

Stock is dead. So is "made in Midjourney."

Five approaches that aren't stock and aren't hand-drawn. Each could be the brand's primary image vocabulary; two of them probably travel together. Each example below is rendered — not described — so you can react to the thing itself.

Concept 01

The receipts.

Documents as imagery

Every image slot is occupied by a real artefact — an audit log row, a redacted regulator citation, a config diff, an evidence hash. Typeset, not photographed. The image is the proof, not a metaphor for the proof.

Cheap Produced by writing.

On voice "Earn every claim" becomes literal.

Defensible CIO can screenshot it into a risk paper.

Density Page reads like a serious document; less suited for hero space.

POLICY · energy.au.aescsf EVIDENCE · r-7f3a · signed
09:41:02 prompt-trace recorded for substation-op-19 in boundary
09:41:03 data egress to _______________ attempted blocked
09:41:03 response routed through tenant-local index ix-au-east-2 in boundary
09:41:04 retention applied · 90 days · per AESCSF §5.2.1 compliant
09:41:04 audit envelope sealed for regulator export signed
sha256 · 9c2f a8e1 04d7 b3f0 … auditor · m.chen@energy.gov.au
Concept 02

The boundary, repeated.

One motif, many scales

A single diagrammatic motif — a rectangle with a green path inside and a red path outside — rendered at every scale the brand needs. Network topology, model lineage, prompt trace, fleet view. The customer learns one shape and reads it everywhere.

Ownable Becomes recognisably Arda within months.

Cheap SVG only. No commissioned work.

Functional Carries product information, not vibes.

Repetition Has to vary enough to not feel like the same picture twice.

Scale · tenant
Scale · fleet
Concept 03

Documentary infrastructure.

Commissioned B&W of real operational sites

A small library of black-and-white documentary photographs — substations at dawn, transformer halls, control rooms, dispatch decks, server cages. Commissioned from one photographer in one trip. Treated with a single navy or accent-tone duotone. Shown below: silhouette placeholder for an actual commissioned image.

Distinctive No competitor has a real photo of a real substation.

Believable Reinforces "we belong here."

Cost Real shoot · ~£15–25k for a useable library.

Access Needs a customer or partner who'll let us in.

PLACEHOLDER · commissioned shoot

The transformer hall at Baraba, NSW. We deploy here.

Treatment · navy duotone, 10% fog
Concept 04

Engineering schematics.

Hairline line-art lifted from real engineering

Single-line electrical diagrams, network topologies, building floorplans, server-rack elevations — rendered as 1px hairline line-art on navy. Functional drawings, not decoration. The buyer recognises the convention; the brand inherits its credibility.

Cheap SVG. Brand asset library, not photography.

Recognised Every engineer in our buying committee reads these for a living.

Cold No human warmth — pair with concept 03 if needed.

Concept 05

The product, in window chrome.

Real screens of Arda Core, Code, Index, Graph

The image slot is the product itself. Arda Core's deployment console, Arda Code's diff view, Arda Graph's lineage tree — captured in a macOS/browser frame and used as the hero. Doubles as marketing and as product demo. Forces us to make the product UI good enough to show.

Honest What you see is what you buy.

Multipurpose Same asset works for deck, web, sales, docs.

Dependency Requires the product UI to actually exist at quality.

Stale Screens age with the product — needs versioning discipline.

4,118
Prompts · 24h
02
Drift signals
PROMPT VOLUME · LAST 7 DAYS · +18%
—   What we need from you

Three decisions. The rest follows.

Each below has a default we'll run with if we don't hear otherwise. Reply inline or on a call.

01
Which palette do we ship?
A · Receipt
B · Sodium
C · Treaty
D · Static

Design default: A · Receipt.

02
Which two imagery concepts travel together?
01 · Receipts (type-as-image)
02 · Boundary motif
03 · B&W documentary
04 · Schematics
05 · Product screens

Design default: 01 + 04 today. Add 03 when budget allows. 05 when the product UI is ready.

03
Are the worm-green / worm-red semantics safe?
Keep · they're load-bearing
Replace · with palette-native pair

Design default: Keep. They earn their saturation by carrying meaning, not decoration.

Arda · v3 direction memo · 15 May 2026 · confidential
Next · palette + imagery lock by 22 May
